title: Discrepancy Feature
excerpt: I want to create a feature that compares two number columns and, if the values differ, sets a status column to “Discrepancy” and moves the item to a Discrepancies group. Can this be done using Monday.com’s native system? …

posts.author: JDAY (Jared Yousif)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to create a feature that compares two number columns and, if the values differ, sets a status column to “Discrepancy” and moves the item to a Discrepancies group. Can this be done using Monday.com’s native system?

For example, if the “Current Count” and “Future Count” columns are unequal, the subitem’s status should change to “Discrepancy,” the parent item’s status should also update to “Discrepancy,” and the item should move to the Discrepancies group.

image1632×797 94.9 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, it can be. The Current Count & Future Count - are those formulas or numbers that are manually added?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: JDAY (Jared Yousif)
posts.cookedHtml:
They are number columns! The current count gets pulled from another board, and the furfure count is manually entered.

Thanks for the reply!

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jared Yousif:

want to create a feature that compares two number columns and, if the values differ, sets a status column to “Discrepancy” and moves the item to a Discrepancies group. Can this be done using Monday.com’s native system?

For example, if the “Current Count” and “Future Count” columns are unequal, the subitem’s status should change to “Discrepancy,” the parent item’s status should also update to “Discrepancy,” and the item should move to the Discrepancies group.

Yes, you can achieve most of this workflow using monday.com’s native automation features, though with some limitations and a bit of creativity. Here’s how you can break it down:

Step-by-Step Native Setup

1. Compare Two Number Columns

Unfortunately, monday.com does not support direct column-to-column comparison in automations. However, you can use a Formula column to do this:

  • Add a Formula column with a formula like:
IF({Current Count} != {Future Count}, "Discrepancy", "Match")

2. Set Status to “Discrepancy”

  • Use an automation:
When formula changes and formula contains "Discrepancy", set status to "Discrepancy"

3. Move Item to “Discrepancies” Group

  • Use another automation:
When status changes to "Discrepancy", move item to group "Discrepancies"

4. Update Parent Item’s Status from Subitem

This part is not natively supported in monday.com. Subitems can’t directly trigger changes in parent items. However, you can:

  • Use a third-party integration like Make.com or Zapier to:
    • Monitor subitem status.
    • If it changes to “Discrepancy,” update the parent item’s status.

Summary of What’s Possible Natively

Feature Native Support Notes
Compare two number columns Use a Formula column
Set status based on formula Via automation
Move item to group Based on status
Update parent from subitem Use Make.com or Zapier

Would you like help walking through it or help generate the logic? Or if this helped let me know with a like!

posts.author: MarkP (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml:
Formula columns in monday can’t be used as automation triggers, no matter which version you’re on. These instructions look like they are AI generated, which could explain the inaccuracy of step 2.

As far as I’m aware, your use case cannot be handled natively. You’ll either have to continue to manage manually or use a third-party app.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JDAY,

It can be easily done with an 3rd-party app called Advanced Formula Booster, as you can see below:

All you need is to create one formula with the following syntax:

2025-07-10_13-38-40616×141 36.9 KB

Syntax Explanation

  1. If both count are equals, stop the formula (therefore the rest of the lines are only executed if there is a discrepancy)
  2. Change the status of the sub-item to “Discrepancy”
  3. Change the status of the parent item to “Discrepancy”
  4. Move the parent item to the “Discrepancies” group.

As you can see, this app allows you to create formulas with multiple lines (without using the formula column), formulas that can update multiple columns from multiple items (here, it modifies not only the current sub-item, but also its parent), formulas that can perform actions like moving items to a new group.

All you need is a single automation:

2025-07-10_13-42-28872×145 18.2 KB

Hope it helps.
